cases.passage: Sahil, a self-employed accountant, has been managing the financial transactions of his bakery for the past three years. He records all the transactions in a journal in chronological order, including both the debit and credit sides. However, to get a clear picture of the financial performance of the bakery, Sahil needs to prepare a trial balance at the end of each month. He also needs to prepare a balance sheet to show the financial position of the bakery at the end of the month. Sahil uses the accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Capital, to ensure that his financial statements are accurate and reliable.

questions.q: Why does Sahil need to prepare a trial balance at the end of each month?
opts: (A) To check for errors in the journal entries
(B) To prepare the financial statements
(C) To ensure accuracy of financial statements
(D) To show the financial position of the bakery
questions.explanation: Sahil needs to prepare a trial balance to ensure accuracy of financial statements.

questions.q: Which of the following accounting equation is used by Sahil?
opts: (A) Assets = Liabilities + Expenses
(B) Assets = Liabilities + Capital
(C) Assets = Liabilities - Capital
(D) Assets = Capital - Liabilities
questions.explanation: Sahil uses the accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Capital, to ensure that his financial statements are accurate and reliable.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of preparing a balance sheet?
opts: (A) To show the financial performance of the bakery
(B) To show the financial position of the bakery
(C) To show the financial expenses of the bakery
(D) To show the financial income of the bakery
questions.explanation: The main purpose of preparing a balance sheet is to show the financial position of the bakery.

opts: (A) A list of all the assets of the bakery
(B) A list of all the liabilities of the bakery
(C) A list of all the revenues and expenses of the bakery
(D) A list of all the debit and credit transactions in chronological order
questions.explanation: A trial balance is a list of all the debit and credit transactions in chronological order.

questions.q: Why does Sahil record all the transactions in a journal in chronological order?
opts: (A) To prepare the financial statements
(B) To check for errors in the journal entries
(C) To ensure accuracy of financial statements
(D) To show the financial position of the bakery
questions.explanation: Sahil records all the transactions in a journal in chronological order to show the financial position of the bakery.

cases.passage: Rahul, a businessman, has been operating a small retail store for the past two years. He prepares a cash book to record all the cash transactions of his store, including receipts and payments. Rahul also prepares a sales day book and a purchases day book to record the sales and purchases of his store. He uses the double-entry system of accounting to record all the transactions in his cash book, sales day book, and purchases day book.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of preparing a sales day book?
opts: (A) To show the financial position of the store
(B) To show the financial performance of the store
(C) To record the sales transactions of the store
(D) To record the purchases transactions of the store
questions.explanation: The main purpose of preparing a sales day book is to record the sales transactions of the store.

questions.q: Why does Rahul prepare a cash book?
opts: (A) To record the sales transactions
(B) To record the purchases transactions
(C) To record all the cash transactions
(D) To show the financial position of the store
questions.explanation: Rahul prepares a cash book to record all the cash transactions of his store, including receipts and payments.

questions.q: What is the double-entry system of accounting?
opts: (A) A system of accounting where only one entry is recorded
(B) A system of accounting where two entries are recorded for each transaction
(C) A system of accounting where no entries are recorded
(D) A system of accounting where all transactions are recorded in a single entry
questions.explanation: The double-entry system of accounting is a system of accounting where two entries are recorded for each transaction.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of preparing a purchases day book?
opts: (A) To show the financial position of the store
(B) To show the financial performance of the store
(C) To record the purchases transactions of the store
(D) To record the sales transactions of the store
questions.explanation: The main purpose of preparing a purchases day book is to record the purchases transactions of the store.

questions.q: Why does Rahul use the double-entry system of accounting?
opts: (A) To simplify the accounting process
(B) To ensure accuracy of financial statements
(C) To reduce the number of entries
(D) To increase the number of entries
questions.explanation: Rahul uses the double-entry system of accounting to ensure accuracy of financial statements.
